title: Undo manager implementation with batch support for scene graph modifications
purpose: Provides undo/redo functionality for scene graph node modifications with support for batching multiple operations into single undoable actions.
module_context: Exports an UndoManager class for managing undo/redo stacks and a factory function for creating property change undo entries.

steps.content: Execute the forward operation of the undo entry.
IF currently in a batch operation, add the entry to the batch.
ELSE add the entry to the undo stack and clear the redo stack.
IF currently in a batch operation, add the entry to the batch.
ELSE add the entry to the undo stack and clear the redo stack.
Pop the most recent entry from the undo stack.
IF no entry exists, return null.
Execute the inverse operation of the popped entry.
Add the popped entry to the redo stack.
Return the label of the undone entry.
Pop the most recent entry from the redo stack.
IF no entry exists, return null.
Execute the forward operation of the popped entry.
Add the popped entry to the undo stack.
Return the label of the redone entry.
Store the provided batch label.
Initialize a new empty array to collect batch entries.
IF no batch entries exist or the batch is empty, reset the batch tracking.
ELSE collect the batch entries and label.
Reset the batch tracking.
Create a new undo entry that executes all forward operations in the batch.
Create a new undo entry that executes all inverse operations in reverse order of the batch.
Add the combined batch entry to the undo stack and clear the redo stack.
Clear the undo stack.
Clear the redo stack.
Reset the batch tracking.

methods.name: createPropertyChange
methods.normalized_params: (node: SceneNode, changes: Partial<SceneNode>, label: string): UndoEntry

steps.content: Capture the current values of the changed properties from the scene node.
Build and return an undo entry with the specified label.
Configure the forward operation to apply the new property values to the scene node.
Configure the inverse operation to restore the original property values to the scene node.
